text.tspan: Padding Strategy for Bank Conflict Avoidance

g.text: Memory Layout Detail (First 8 Columns of Rows 0-3)

text.tspan: With padding: consecutive rows shift bank assignment by +1
Column access: thread[row] reads tile[row][col] → Bank = (row×33 + col) % 32
All threads access DIFFERENT banks → NO CONFLICTS
When accessing column x in tile[32][32]:
ALL 32 threads access Bank x → 32-way conflict!
Each row shifts by +1 → ALL threads access DIFFERENT banks!
← Column 1 access: Row 0→B1, Row 1→B2, Row 2→B3, Row 3→B4
Each thread → DIFFERENT bank → NO CONFLICTS!
32 banks × 4 bytes wide = 128 bytes per bank row
Successive 4-byte words map to successive banks (0→1→2→...→31→0)

g.text: Bank assignment wraps: byte 128 → Bank 0, byte 132 → Bank 1, etc.

text.tspan: // WITHOUT padding - 32-way bank conflict on column access
// Each row shifted by +1 bank relative to previous row
// WITHOUT padding - 32-way bank conflict on column access
// Each row shifted by +1 bank relative to previous row
